senses.definition: an object shaped like a samara (a winged seed) or resembling a seed that can spin or glide when released.
examples: The toy was designed as a samaroid and glided softly through the air.
Researchers noted the samaroid structure aided in wind dispersal.

senses.definition: the capital of the ancient kingdom of Assyria; also a city in modern Iraq known for historical and archaeological significance.
examples: Samarra features in ancient Mesopotamian history.
Archaeologists conducted excavations near Samarra.

senses.definition: a radioactive mineral containing rare earth elements and uranium, used in scientific and industrial contexts.
examples: Samarskite samples were analyzed for their yttrium content.
The mineral is valued for its complex chemistry in research.

senses.definition: plural of samarskite; multiple samples of the mineral.
examples: The museum displayed several samarskites from different mines.
Geologists compared samarskites to study variations.

senses.definition: a term used in some culinary or cultural contexts to refer to a type of bread or flatbread? (Note: common lexicographic usage may vary; here treated as a plural form with uncertain standard sense.)
examples: Local cooks prepared several samas for the festival.

senses.definition: a Brazilian dance and musical style with energetic, rhythmic movements.
examples: They performed a lively samba at the parade.
Samba rhythms make many people want to dance.

senses.definition: to dance to samba music; to perform samba.
examples: We sambaed all night at the party.
They sambaed with joy along the street.

senses.definition: the past tense of samba; danced to samba music.
examples: She sambaed until dawn at the festival.
They sambaed along the pier with bright smiles.

senses.definition: present participle of samba; dancing to samba music.
examples: They were sambaing in the square after sunset.
People started sambaing as the band began to play.

senses.definition: a spicy chili paste used in Southeast Asian cuisine.
examples: The curry had a kick from the sambal sauce.
Sambal can be very hot depending on the recipe.

senses.definition: smaller or various forms of the same chili paste used as condiments.
examples: They offered different sambals to suit tastes.
A dollop of sambal elevated the dish.

senses.definition: plural of samba; multiple dance performances or songs in the samba style.
examples: The festival featured several sambas from different schools.

senses.definition: to perform a samba while walking or moving; to march in a samba style.
examples: The troupe sambathe down the avenue during the parade.

senses.definition: a Southeast Asian chili sauce similar to sambal; may be used as a variant spelling.
examples: The dish was served with a fiery sambel on the side.

senses.definition: a South Indian lentil-based vegetable stew seasoned with sambar powder; a staple dish.
examples: We had hot sambhar with fluffy idlis for breakfast.

senses.definition: plural of sambhar; multiple lentil-based stews.
examples: The menu offered several sambhars from different regions.

senses.definition: in Buddhist cosmology, a form of the Buddha that is accessible to beings in the world; a
aspect.
examples: The statue represents the sambhogakaya manifestation.

senses.definition: a historical or variant spelling related to Hindu or Buddhist terms; specific meaning depends on context.
examples: In ancient texts, sambhur appears as a phonetic variant.

senses.definition: plural of sambhur; multiple instances of the above.
examples: The manuscript mentions several sambhurs.

senses.definition: a Brazilian martial art that combines elements of dance, acrobatics, and music; also a term used historically for a person of mixed African and Indigenous ancestry in some regions (note: sensitive terminology; context matters).
examples: He practices capoeira, not sambo.

senses.definition: plural of sambo; multiple instances or uses of the martial art or term.
examples: Historical texts list several samboes.
